assessments.due_date:
Week 1 - Week 13

Due Dates: Internship: Weeks 1-13; Course meetings: Weeks 2, 5, 7, 9 and 12; Peer review: Week 9 meeting.
assessments.task_description:
Due Dates: Internship: Weeks 1-13; Course meetings: Weeks 2, 5, 7, 9 and 12; Peer review: Week 9 meeting.

Word count: variable

Task Instructions: The Management of Learning tasks comprise 3 components:

·        Completion of tasks set by the internship supervisor (20 marks): This component involves liaising with the placement supervisor, proactively seeking out work, showing initiative for periods when there are no specific tasks set by the supervisor, completion of tasks, and reporting back to the supervisor. Students are expected to behave professionally at all times. If they are unable to attend the workplace for any reason, they must contact their supervisor to let them know and provide an explanation. The course coordinator will consult with students’ supervisors at the end of semester for allocating a mark for this component.

·        Active participation in meetings (10 marks): Students are expected to attend and actively participate in the 5 course meetings (weeks 2, 5, 7, 9 and 12). Activities will include discussion of internship progress, small group work, and scaffolding tasks.

·        Peer review of scaffolding tasks for Placement Report Essay (10 marks):

This component involves bringing a draft abstract and a 1 page outline of the proposed report to the week 9 meeting and providing constructive feedback to peers on their draft abstracts and outlines.

Criteria:

  • Completion of tasks set by the internship supervisor in a timely way; organisational skills; a professional and courteous attitude to supervisors; and having a proactive attitude to the internship. (20 marks)
  • Active participation in meetings. Students are expected to initiate discussion, engage with their peers, and share ideas and experiences. (10 marks)
  • Peer review: providing constructive feedback in a collegial way on the draft abstracts and outlines of other students in the group (10 marks).

 

Secure Assessment: This assessment is designed to protect academic integrity and assess your understanding of course concepts. You must only use the approved materials and tools for this assessment task. Use of prohibited materials in this task may be investigated and treated as academic misconduct. See assessment instructions for more details.

AI and MT:

·        Completion of tasks set by the internship supervisor (secure assessment):

Students should only use AI/MT on tasks set by the internship supervisor if expressly requested. Students are expected to conform to the AI policy of the organisation at which they undertake their placement.

·        Library Materials and Gen-AI: Students are also reminded that library materials may not be entered into AI technologies without express permission: Policies and guidelines - Library - The University of Queensland

“You may not share, re-publish, copy or enter into AI technologies any part of a resource accessed from our online collections unless expressly permitted in the licence terms for that resource”

“Except as permitted under each Publisher's terms and conditions of use, you must not:

Use UQ Library resource content as an input to generative AI products or services”


For referencing AI/MT see the Library guide: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/ai-tools-assignments

For referencing AI in MLA style see: How do I cite generative AI in MLA style? from the official MLA style website.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Participate in person in class and at internship (unless otherwise arranged with supervisor).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

Due Dates: Weeks 2-11 Due by Friday 4pm of the relevant week. Responses due by 4pm the following Monday.
assessments.task_description:
Word count: 150 words each journal post plus 2 responses (1-2 sentences each)

Task Instructions: Students will post weekly reflections on the internship and respond briefly to the posts of others. Upload a 150-word post each week (weeks 2-11), by Friday 4pm, to the Learn@UQ ENGL3020 course site padlet. The entries may, for example: record tasks, reflect on experiences and challenges, critically evaluate the aims and progress of the project, or reflect more broadly on the aims and methods of the type of work that the project represents and how it is situated within the humanities. In addition to uploading your own journal post, students are expected to respond briefly (1-2 sentences) to 2 posts from others each week. [Responses due by 4pm the following Monday.]

Criteria:

  • Journal posts: completion of 10 weekly posts (weeks 2-11) (approx. 150 words each) evidencing reflection on the internship (10 marks)
  • Response (1-2 sentences) to the posts of others (2 per week) (5 marks)

 

Open Assessment: In this assessment you can use resources such as your notes, course materials, and other tools, including AI, while completing the task. The focus is not on memorising information, but on understanding, applying judgement, and demonstrating your own thinking.

Note that, while this assessment is classified as open assessment, journal posts are more likely to evidence genuine reflection if the task is not outsourced to AI. Also note that this task does not judge the quality of the writing but only whether the posts reflect genuine reflection. If AI/MT is used, it must be referenced in each post.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission instructions: Submit journal posts via the padlet [link on course site].
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Word count: 1750 words, excluding Works Cited list.

Task Instructions: The student will write a report in response to their internship experience. The report will comprise:

·        Abstract (250 words) providing an overview of the report.

·        Introduction (250 words) to the organisation/placement of their internship and its contexts within the humanities industry.

·        Tasks (250 words): an outline of the tasks and activities undertaken as part of the internship.

·        Reflection (500 words) on the placement experience, drawing from the journal entries and at least 1 secondary source on reflective practice.

·        Future directions (250 words): ideas on potential future projects for the organisation/placement, or suggested improvements to procedures or practices (eg on sustainability, inclusion, First Nations research methodology, community outreach, ethical practices etc). This section should include at least 1 secondary source relevant to your ideas or the contexts of your organisation/placement.

·        Transferable skills (250 words): an outline of your skills and abilities that you could include in your cv or for a job application.

Style guide: MLA style. 

Criteria:

  • Abstract providing concise and coherent overview of the report (5 marks)
  • Clarity and coherence of report content (20 marks)
  • Thoughtful reflection on the experiences of the internship and the project (10 marks)
  • Writing mechanics and expression; presentation and MLA style (10 marks)


Open Assessment: In this assessment you can use resources such as your notes, course materials, and other tools, including AI, while completing the task. The focus is not on memorising information, but on understanding, applying judgement, and demonstrating your own thinking.

Note that:

·        while this assessment is classified as open assessment, so the use of AI is permissible, students should be aware that the use of AI may result in errors and fabricated information and sources. If the assessment submission contains fabricated materials, the submission may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct and will be submitted for integrity review.

·        If AI/MT is used, it must be acknowledged on the cover sheet and referenced in the Works Cited list.

·        Students should be capable of demonstrating detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

Library Materials and Gen-AI: Students are also reminded that library materials may not be entered into AI technologies without express permission: Policies and guidelines - Library - The University of Queensland

“You may not share, re-publish, copy or enter into AI technologies any part of a resource accessed from our online collections unless expressly permitted in the licence terms for that resource”

“Except as permitted under each Publisher's terms and conditions of use, you must not:

Use UQ Library resource content as an input to generative AI products or services”


For referencing AI/MT see the Library guide: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/ai-tools-assignments

For referencing AI in MLA style see: How do I cite generative AI in MLA style? from the official MLA style website.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via Turnitin using the assessment tab in the Learn@UQ site. The link will become available the day before the due date.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Internship

This component involves liaising with the placement supervisor, proactively seeking out work, showing initiative for periods when there are no specific tasks set by the supervisor, completion of tasks, and reporting back to the supervisor. 


Learning outcomes: L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Internship This component involves liaising with the placement supervisor, proactively seeking out work, showing initiative for periods when there are no specific tasks set by the supervisor, completion of tasks, and reporting back to the supervisor.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 2 Meeting 1

  • Discussion: Introduction to the course and the cohort;
  • Small group work: expectations of the internship: tasks, skills and challenges
  • Mini-lecture: course assessment requirements; time management ;
  • Small group work: our transferable skills; and
  • Discussion: journalling techniques.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 2 Meeting 1 Discussion: Introduction to the course and the cohort; Small group work : expectations of the internship: tasks, skills and challenges Mini-lecture : course assessment requirements; time management ; Small group work : our transferable skills; and Discussion : journalling techniques.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 5 Meeting 2

  • Discussion of internships and progress on journals;
  • Small group work: introduce your placement and its contexts to the cohort;
  • Mini-lecture on scaffolding techniques for projects in literary studies;
  • Demo: on using the databases for research and discussion of scholarly sources;
  • Small group work: workshop ideas for placement report essay

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 5 Meeting 2 Discussion of internships and progress on journals; Small group work : introduce your placement and its contexts to the cohort; Mini-lecture on scaffolding techniques for projects in literary studies; Demo : on using the databases for research and discussion of scholarly sources; Small group work : workshop ideas for placement report essay

learning_activities.topic:
Week 7 Meeting 3

·        Discussion of internships and progress on journals;

·        Mini-lecture on creativity techniques as scaffolding for research projects in literary studies: mind maps, creative journalling, zines.

·        Activity: create a zine or 3-D essay plan for your Placement Report essay.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 7 Meeting 3 · Discussion of internships and progress on journals; · Mini-lecture on creativity techniques as scaffolding for research projects in literary studies: mind maps, creative journalling, zines. · Activity: create a zine or 3-D essay plan for your Placement Report essay.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 9 Meeting 4

  • Discussion on progress of internships;
  • Workshop: peer review of scaffolding tasks for Placement Report essay (draft abstract and 1 page outline); and
  • Discussion on requirements for Placement Report essay.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 9 Meeting 4 Discussion on progress of internships; Workshop : peer review of scaffolding tasks for Placement Report essay (draft abstract and 1 page outline); and Discussion on requirements for Placement Report essay.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 12 Meeting 5

  • Discussion: update on final stages of the internships;
  • Check-in: any final questions on the Placement Report essay;
  • Discussion: reflections on the internships: skills, experiences and challenges.
  • Activity: draft a paragraph for your cv or application for a dream job, including skills you’ve developed this semester. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
